status_detail: 100 MW Alkaline electrolysis project in Portugal, developed by EDP and Martifer. PEM and Alkaline options being evaluated. Part of the Portuguese PNEC 2030 strategy targeting industrial decarbonization. Focus on ammonia and refinery offtake.

data_management.narrative_summary: Green H2 Atlantic is a 100 MW alkaline electrolysis project in Portugal developed by a partnership between EDP and Martifer (MPR). The project targets green hydrogen production for industrial decarbonization in Portugal, focusing on ammonia and refinery offtake. Portugal's National Hydrogen Strategy (PNEC 2030) targets 2.5 GW of electrolysis capacity by 2030, and this project is part of a portfolio of development projects in the Sines area, leveraging deep-water port infrastructure, abundant solar/wind resources, and existing industrial hydrogen demand.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Sines industrial zone with dedicated renewables

offtake.offtaker_name: Industrial users at Sines industrial zone

sources.title: Green H2 Atlantic — EDP/Martifer 100 MW hydrogen project Portugal
